title: Quantify '10x' productivity claims or remove them
summary: AI-generated copy uses '10x' multiplier claims without defining the baseline or measurement methodology.
instruction: When claiming a performance multiplier, state the baseline and measurement: 'Teams review code 3x faster (from 4 hours to 80 minutes average)' rather than unqualified '10x productivity.'
rationale: A '10x' claim without a baseline is meaningless — 10x of what? Zero? Industry average? The previous tool? Unsupported multiplier claims erode credibility when users test them against reality. A specific before/after comparison is both more honest and more persuasive.

violations: '10x your team's productivity' with no baseline or measurement methodology.
'5x faster deployment' compared to nothing specified.
controls: 'Teams using our tool complete code reviews 3x faster (from 4 hours to 80 minutes on average, based on 500+ teams).'
'2x revenue growth for e-commerce merchants using our checkout optimization (source: merchant analytics, n=1,200 stores).'
borderline: A 10x improvement over a known industry baseline that does not need restating (e.g., '10x faster than disk I/O' is meaningful to the audience).

exceptions.description: Performance claims against well-known industry baselines where the baseline is implicit. Scientific or engineering contexts where '10x' has a defined meaning.

detector.signalDescription: Find 'Nx' or 'N-times' productivity or performance claims. Check for comparative baselines ('than', 'vs', 'from') or measurement methodology nearby.
limitations: Cannot verify the accuracy of baseline claims.
Some well-known industry baselines are implicit and legitimately do not need restating.

scenarios: A well-known comparison where the baseline is standard knowledge (e.g., '10x faster than TCP').
Academic or engineering contexts where multiplier claims have defined methodologies.
mitigations: Accept 'than [known baseline]' patterns as sufficient. Flag only standalone multiplier claims without any comparison reference.

copyPattern.replacementStrategy: Replace unqualified 'Nx' claims with specific before/after comparisons: 'From [baseline] to [result] — an Nx improvement measured by [methodology].'

fixturePlan.description: A landing page claiming '10x your productivity' with no baseline or methodology.
Same page with 'Teams ship features 3x faster — from bi-weekly to daily deployments (based on 200+ team survey).'
A dev tool claiming '10x faster than git' where git performance is a known baseline.
